Vue3.basic:
<mc-card
  variant="borderless"
  orientation="horizontal"
  fit="small"
  heading="Supply Chain and Logistics"
  subheading="Integrated logistics"
  body="value"
  footer="12 September 2022"
  contentalignment="middle"
  padding="value"
  image="value"
  imagepercent="value"
  imagescalestrength="medium"
  imagebackgroundcolor="value"
  href="value"
  rel="value"
  target="value"
  clickable
  actions="value"
  hasimageslot
  hassubheadingslot
  hasbodyslot
  hasfooterslot
  hasactionsslot>
</mc-card>

props.name: variant
props.description: Description missing

props.name: orientation
props.description: Description missing

props.name: fit
props.description: Description missing

props.name: heading
props.description: Heading

props.name: subheading
props.description: Sub heading can be passed as simple argument or as a default slot

props.name: body
props.defaultValue: We focus on solving your supply chain needs from end to end, taking the complexity out of container shipping for you.
props.description:
Body can be passed as simple argument like: `body="simple text"` or as a default slot: `
body text as HTML
`

props.name: footer
props.description:
Footer can be passed as simple argument like: `footer="simple text"` or as a named slot: `
footer text as HTML
`

props.name: contentalignment
props.description: How content (heading, subheading, body and footer) is aligned.

props.name: padding
props.description: Padding around all of the card's content.

props.name: image
props.description: Image URL

props.name: imagepercent
props.description:
For vertical cards, this is the height of the image as a percentage of the card width, defaults to a 16:9 ratio (height = 56.25%)



For horizontal cards, this is the width of the image as a percentage of the card width, defaults to 33%.

props.name: imagescalestrength
props.description: Strength of the scale effect on the image.

props.name: imagebackgroundcolor
props.description: The background colour to display "under" the image as a fallback

props.name: href
props.description: Passing a href value will render the card as a HTML anchor element.

props.name: rel
props.description: The relationship of the linked URL as space-separated link types.

props.name: target
props.description: Where to display the linked URL, as the name for a browsing context (a tab, window, or <iframe>).

props.name: clickable
props.description: If set, the card will get mouseover effects and a pointer cursor. You can then listen to the `click` event to handle the click event, optionally handle you application router and redirects.

props.name: actions
props.description:
There is one slot available called`actions`. This can be used for adding links / buttons etc.



The `actions` slot will not be rendered if the Card component has a `href`.




For more details on usage, please look at the Code Preview above.

props.name: hasimageslot
props.description:
**For server-side rendering only (e.g., Next.js).**


Explicitly set to `true` when using the image slot to ensure it renders during SSR hydration. On client-side rendering, the image slot is detected automatically and this property is not needed.

props.name: hassubheadingslot
props.description:
**For server-side rendering only (e.g., Next.js).**


Explicitly set to `true` when using the subheading slot to ensure it renders during SSR hydration. On client-side rendering, the subheading slot is detected automatically and this property is not needed.

props.name: hasbodyslot
props.description:
**For server-side rendering only (e.g., Next.js).**


Set to true when providing content to the default body slot. Not needed for client-side rendering as the slot is detected automatically.

props.name: hasfooterslot
props.description:
**For server-side rendering only (e.g., Next.js).**


Set to true when providing content to footer/action slots. Not needed for client-side rendering as the slot is detected automatically.

props.name: hasactionsslot
props.description:
**For server-side rendering only (e.g., Next.js).**


Explicitly set to `true` when using the actions slot to ensure it renders during SSR hydration. On client-side rendering, the actions slot is detected automatically and this property is not needed.

slots.description: Use the default slot, when you want to pass body as HTML. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
Use the subheading named slot, when you want to pass subheading as HTML. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: subheading

slots.description: Use the actions named slot, when you want to render action buttons or links at the bottom of the card. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: actions

slots.description: Use the image named slot, when you want to pass image as HTML. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: image

slots.description: Use the footer named slot, when you want to render footer content as HTML. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: footer

guidelines.title: Card
guidelines.description: A card is a focused way of displaying content and actions on a specific topic.

general: Keep the card simple so the message is easy to understand
Make sure that the text inside the card is concise and easy to read
Avoid overloading the card with too many actions and/or links
If the whole card is clickable then make sure it is easy to predict what is happening when clicked
Cards work well as a teaser for e.g. news articles or blog posts
Cards are a good way to display related topics
On desktop, cards work well in a column-based layout e.g. 3 or 4 cards in a row
Do not stretch a card across the entire page

designPrinciples.title: Spacing
designPrinciples.description: Consistent spacing creates groups, rhythm and good scan-ability.

use-4-pixel-spacing: The base unit for spacing is 4 pixels, subsequent units are multiples of 4 (such as 8, 12, 16, etc.). These units should be used consistently for all paddings, margins, gutters, typography, and layouts to ensure a cohesive appearance and user experience across all Maersk digital products.
exceptions-to-4-pixel-spacing: When creating components, finer control is needed for spacing elements. Three acceptable exceptions here are 2, 6, and 10 pixels.
components: Relate the component elements and still keep them distinct by using space units dervided from the 4-pixel base spacing. These space units will make aligning and using non-core components with core ones easier.
patterns: A recognisable pattern is created when consistent spacing is used to establish a visual rhythm and relationship between the pattern elements.

Pattern spacing needs to be coherent which means not all space units need to be the same. Several spacing units can help establish better visual balance when dealing with differently-sized UI elements in the pattern.
layouts: Visual hierarchy in layouts requires well spaced regions and UI elements. Spacing units can control the information density of any application layout. Use them to provide the right level for the context and users' expectation.
information-density: Space is the element that controls the information density of any interface. Adjusting the space between blocks on information creates a visual rhythm and hierarchy.

High information density can be disorienting and overwhelming if spacing does not allow the eyes to rest and let people process what they see. People may notice the grouped elements but not process each individual item.

We recommend reading the <PageLink path="/design-language/component-fit/">Fit guidelines</PageLink> to help you make an informed decision what spacing units will be most suitable for your layout composition.

designPrinciples.title: Corner radius
designPrinciples.description: "Corner radius refers to the curvature of the corners on elements like cards, buttons, notifications etc."

nested-components: For components that contain other components, it is important to ensure that the outer component has a larger corner radius than the nested component. This approach creates a visually appealing and balanced spacing between the two elements.

All components come with a default border radius applied and are designed to work harmoniously in most situations. However, there may be special cases where you need to manually adjust the border radius of a nested component. In the table below, you can view all the available values.
values:
| Name | Value |
|------|-------|
| radius-xs | 2px |
| radius-sm | 4px |
| radius-md | 6px |
| radius-lg | 8px |
| radius-xl | 12px |
| radius-2xl | 16px |
| radius-full | 9999px |
2xl-radius-16px: Used for elements such as large images, dialogs, or modals.
xl-radius-12px: Used for components such as cards, which frequently contain nested components.
lg-radius-8px: Used for components such as notifications, toasts, popovers, and tables.
md-radius-6px: Elements such as buttons and input fields.
